title: Decision Fatigue

content.question: Which repeating decision drains you daily that you could systemize?
content.life_lens: Remove decisions: what to eat, wear.
sop: Track every decision you make for one full day, no matter how small.
Rate your Energy 1-10 and count total Decisions made that day (see calculation, page 5)
Identify the 3 decisions that repeat daily and add zero real value (what to wear, eat, etc.).
Turn each into a standing rule, template, or default so it's never re-decided.
Schedule your highest-stakes decisions for the first half of the day, before fatigue sets in.
questions: Which repeating decision drains you daily that you could systemize?
What was the quality of your decisions at 4pm today compared to 9am?
How many trivial decisions have you already made today before doing anything that actually matters?

definitions.def: The drop in decision quality after making many decisions.

content.equation_example: Rate Energy 1-10 and count Decisions (number made that day). Energy 8 divided by Decisions 40 equals 0.2 quality score. Higher means your remaining decisions are still sharp. Lower means you've burned through your decision-making energy and every choice after this point is getting worse.
content.closing_statement: Every trivial decision spends capacity you'll need later.

caption:
Principle Nº 134: Decision Fatigue

Too many decisions = worse decisions.

Ask yourself: Which repeating decision drains you daily that you could systemize?
